title: Itertools, Infinite Streams, and Bounded Pipelines · Functional Systems Mission
summary: A facilities team receives an open-ended sensor event stream. Produce rolling summaries with bounded memory, cancellation, and malformed-record evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Stream Monitor with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.
Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.
Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.
